enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about a late-night work session mapping Roman roads and aqueducts onto Colorado geography as part of a personal project building a Roman Empire analog. He describes his process of tracing Via Appia and other roads using Google Earth and Wikipedia, and plans to continue mapping tribes and towns that day.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Excited and confident — believes the whole Roman Empire can be built out within a month
Recognizes a clear two-stage fatigue pattern: physical tiredness first, then mental decline, and respects it
Finds value in hearing different people explain history — variety of voices helps memorization

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He traced a bunch of trash in Boulder County.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He started building and tracing aqueducts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He traced the roads by looking at Via Appia and comparing distances.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He traced a road from Boulder to Shiprock and called it the Appian Road.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He found a town where the Appian Road breaks off and traced its distance from Boulder and Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He traced another road up north but couldn't decide between northeast or far west.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He decided to go farther west because the east would be equivalent to the sea and going to India.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He got physically and mentally tired after working for a long time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He noticed that physical tiredness precedes mental tiredness.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He found a new YouTube channel called Extra Credits that talks about the Punic Wars and the Gracchi.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He thinks listening to different history teachers on YouTube helps with memorization.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to trace the rest of the Roman roads today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes tracing the roads makes it easier to plug in the towns using Wikipedia.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He thinks he can build the whole Roman Empire within a couple of weeks.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He jokes that Rome wasn't built in a day, but in a month.
